note: Peptide-peptide (cross-agent) interaction layer. Derived from a physician-built, citation-audited research library and re-keyed to the AllyOS formulary. Each agent carries a 'stocked' flag: only rules involving at least one stocked (formulary) agent fire in the POC/Ally; non-stocked agents are retained as informational and never produce a recommendation. Grades A-D. 'direct_combination_evidence' false = inference, not a trial. No doses, no prescribing, no regulatory status. Decision-support only.

grades.A: Direct human trial/pharmacology or strong human physiology for the specific agent/pathway.
grades.B: Human mechanistic evidence or well-supported physiology; direct combo evidence may be absent.
grades.C: Preclinical/cell/animal evidence with plausible mechanism; clinical transfer uncertain.
grades.D: Sparse, conflicting, legacy, or formulation-transfer evidence; low-confidence flags only.

actions.FLAG_FOR_PROVIDER_REVIEW: Surface to the treating provider for review; never an autonomous decision.
actions.LANGUAGE_GUARDRAIL: Evidence-quality / identity caution; force uncertainty language.

rules.name: GLP-1 + amylin complementary satiety/GI interaction
rules.class_scope: Any GLP-1 receptor agonist or dual/triple incretin plus amylin agonist
rules.confidence: high for CagriSema; moderate/low for extrapolated pairs
rules.evidence_basis: GLP-1 and amylin pathways both reduce intake and affect gastric/glucagon physiology; cagrilintide+semaglutide has direct human combination evidence, while other GLP/amylin pairings require inference.
rules.ai_action: Flag as potentially complementary for satiety/weight biology but also flag additive GI slowing/nausea/vomiting and glycemic interaction risk when insulin/secretagogues are present.
hard_limits: Mark all non-direct combinations as inference

rules.name: GLP-1 receptor agonist redundancy
rules.class_scope: Any two agents sharing GLP1R agonism
rules.confidence: high mechanistic; low direct clinical for stacking
rules.evidence_basis: GLP1R agonism is already present in each agent; stacking GLP1R agonists has receptor and adverse-signal redundancy unless a direct combination trial says otherwise.
rules.ai_action: Detect duplicate GLP1R agonism and mark as redundant receptor load; do not infer additive benefit.
hard_limits: Mark all non-direct combinations as inference

rules.name: Tirzepatide-retatrutide overlap
rules.class_scope: Dual GIP/GLP-1 plus triple GIP/GLP-1/glucagon agonist
rules.evidence_basis: Retatrutide shares GIPR and GLP1R with tirzepatide and adds GCGR agonism; no direct stack evidence identified.
rules.ai_action: Mark as overlapping incretin receptor load with added glucagon-axis uncertainty; do not infer superiority.
hard_limits: Mark all non-direct combinations as inference

rules.name: Retatrutide with any GLP-1/amylin agent
rules.class_scope: Retatrutide plus GLP1R or amylin pathway agents
rules.evidence_basis: Retatrutide trial reports GI and heart-rate signals; GLP/amylin agents also carry GI satiety/gastric effects. Direct retatrutide-amylin or retatrutide-GLP stack evidence is not established here.
rules.ai_action: Require 'no direct evidence found' annotation and flag GI/heart-rate/adverse-signal overlap.
hard_limits: Mark all non-direct combinations as inference

rules.name: AOD-9604 vs GH-secretagogue distinction
rules.class_scope: hGH fragment plus GH-axis secretagogues
rules.evidence_basis: AOD9604 preclinical work suggests a lipolytic hGH-fragment pathway not mediated by hGH receptor in that model, whereas GH secretagogues act through GH/IGF-axis. Human combo evidence is absent.
rules.ai_action: Do not call AOD a GH secretagogue. Mark combo with GH-axis agents as mechanistically distinct but clinically unvalidated.
hard_limits: Mark all non-direct combinations as inference

rules.name: GHRH analog + GHSR agonist GH synergy
rules.class_scope: GHRH receptor agonist plus ghrelin/GHSR agonist
rules.evidence_basis: Human ghrelin plus GHRH data show synergistic GH release; applying to synthetic GHRH analogs plus GHSR agonists is class inference.
rules.ai_action: Flag possible GH/IGF amplification and downstream glucose/growth-factor monitoring concepts; label as inference unless exact pair has direct data.
hard_limits: Mark all non-direct combinations as inference

rules.name: GHRH analog redundancy
rules.class_scope: Any two GHRH receptor agonists
rules.evidence_basis: All act primarily at GHRH receptor/pituitary GH axis; CJC and tesamorelin evidence both show GH/IGF-axis activation.
rules.ai_action: Mark as redundant GHRH receptor load; do not infer additional benefit.
hard_limits: Mark all non-direct combinations as inference

rules.name: Nonselective GHRP endocrine off-target overlap
rules.class_scope: GHSR agonists, especially GHRP-2/GHRP-6/hexarelin
rules.evidence_basis: GHRP-2/GHRP-6/hexarelin can increase ACTH/cortisol/prolactin, whereas ipamorelin appears more selective in available models.
rules.ai_action: Differentiate ipamorelin from GHRP-2/GHRP-6/hexarelin; flag ACTH/cortisol/prolactin overlap for nonselective GHRPs.
hard_limits: Mark all non-direct combinations as inference

rules.name: GH/IGF axis vs insulin sensitivity
rules.class_scope: GH-axis agents plus metabolic insulin-sensitizing/satiety agents
rules.evidence_basis: GH can oppose insulin action through lipolysis/FFA and insulin signaling pathways, while GLP-1 and MOTS-c literature supports glucose/insulin-sensitivity biology through other mechanisms.
rules.ai_action: When GH-axis and metabolic agents appear together, mark glucose/insulin signal as mixed rather than automatically synergistic.
hard_limits: Mark all non-direct combinations as inference

rules.name: GH/IGF/proliferation pathway load
rules.class_scope: GH/IGF axis plus direct IGF/MGF/telomere-axis agents
rules.confidence: moderate for GH/IGF biology; low for epitalon-specific interaction
rules.evidence_basis: GH/IGF and IGF-1 receptor biology are growth-promoting; epitalon/telomere claims remain weak but should trigger theoretical pro-growth caution in an AI agent.
rules.ai_action: Flag as theoretical growth-factor/telomerase pathway load; do not state proven cancer risk from a specific combination.
hard_limits: Mark all non-direct combinations as inference

rules.name: MOTS-c + GLP/amylin metabolic convergence
rules.class_scope: Mitochondrial metabolic peptide plus incretin/amylin agents
rules.evidence_basis: MOTS-c preclinical metabolic effects converge conceptually with incretin/amylin weight/glucose axes, but direct combination evidence is absent.
rules.ai_action: Mark as convergent metabolic biology with no direct combo evidence; avoid benefit claims.
hard_limits: Mark all non-direct combinations as inference

rules.name: SS-31 + MOTS-c/Humanin mitochondrial convergence
rules.class_scope: Mitochondrial membrane/cytoprotective/metabolic peptides
rules.evidence_basis: SS-31 acts at mitochondrial membranes, MOTS-c is a mitochondrial-derived metabolic signal, and Humanin is cytoprotective; no direct stack evidence identified.
rules.ai_action: Represent as different mitochondrial nodes; do not collapse into one 'mitochondrial booster' mechanism.
hard_limits: Mark all non-direct combinations as inference

rules.name: Epitalon with growth-factor or mitochondrial agents
rules.class_scope: Epitalon/telomere-axis plus pro-growth or mitochondrial agents
rules.evidence_basis: Epitalon evidence is limited/heterogeneous; interaction with GH/IGF or mitochondrial peptides is theoretical and unsupported by direct studies.
rules.ai_action: Force low-confidence annotation. Do not let the AI agent produce strong longevity claims.
hard_limits: Mark all non-direct combinations as inference

rules.name: BPC-157 + TB4/TB-500 + GHK-Cu tissue-repair stack
rules.class_scope: Repair/angiogenesis/collagen remodeling peptides
rules.evidence_basis: These agents converge on wound/tissue remodeling, collagen/fibroblast, cell migration and angiogenesis-related biology. Direct combination evidence is not established and much is preclinical.
rules.ai_action: Flag possible repair-pathway overlap and angiogenesis signal; label clinical synergy as unproven.
hard_limits: Mark all non-direct combinations as inference

rules.name: BPC-157 and NO/angiogenesis interaction context
rules.class_scope: BPC-157 plus angiogenesis/wound-healing peptides
rules.evidence_basis: BPC-157 literature links to NO-system and angiogenesis; TB4/GHK/LL-37 also have wound or angiogenesis/immunomodulatory biology.
rules.ai_action: Add angiogenesis/remodeling overlap tag. Do not claim tumor promotion as proven; mark theoretical only when relevant.
hard_limits: Mark all non-direct combinations as inference

rules.name: KPV + LL-37 inflammatory-direction contrast
rules.class_scope: Anti-inflammatory melanocortin-derived peptide plus antimicrobial immunomodulatory peptide
rules.evidence_basis: KPV is anti-inflammatory in murine colitis models; LL-37 can be antimicrobial/wound-healing but also participates in autoimmune inflammation contexts.
rules.ai_action: Mark immune direction as context-dependent, not simply synergistic or antagonistic.
hard_limits: Mark all non-direct combinations as inference

rules.name: Thymosin alpha-1 + LL-37 immune modulation overlap
rules.class_scope: Adaptive/innate immunomodulatory peptides
rules.evidence_basis: Tα1 modulates immune function; LL-37 is antimicrobial and immunomodulatory with autoimmune-inflammatory context evidence.
rules.ai_action: Flag immune-context dependence and autoimmune/inflammatory phenotype caution; no generic 'immune boost' claim.
hard_limits: Mark all non-direct combinations as inference

rules.name: Thymosin alpha-1 + KPV immune-direction balance
rules.class_scope: Immune modulation plus anti-inflammatory peptide
rules.evidence_basis: Tα1 immune modulation and KPV anti-inflammatory effects can point in different immunologic directions depending on disease model.
rules.ai_action: Represent as context-dependent immune modulation, not automatic synergy.
hard_limits: Mark all non-direct combinations as inference

rules.name: PT-141 + Melanotan II melanocortin redundancy
rules.class_scope: Melanocortin receptor agonists
rules.evidence_basis: Both are melanocortin agonists; MT-II has broader MC receptor activity while PT-141/bremelanotide is primarily reasoned through CNS melanocortin sexual-desire pathways.
rules.ai_action: Flag melanocortin receptor redundancy and additive nausea/flushing/autonomic/pigmentation-signal possibilities; no benefit stacking assumption.
hard_limits: Mark all non-direct combinations as inference

rules.name: PT-141 + Kisspeptin complementary arousal vs HPG-axis
rules.class_scope: Melanocortin sexual-desire pathway plus upstream HPG axis peptide
rules.evidence_basis: PT-141 is CNS melanocortin/arousal biology; kisspeptin stimulates GnRH/LH axis. They are mechanistically distinct but direct combination evidence was not established.
rules.ai_action: Mark as complementary theoretical central-arousal plus HPG-axis, not proven clinical synergy.
hard_limits: Mark all non-direct combinations as inference

rules.name: Kisspeptin + Gonadorelin upstream/downstream redundancy
rules.class_scope: KISS1R agonism plus GnRH receptor agonism
rules.confidence: high physiologic; low direct stack outcome
rules.evidence_basis: Kisspeptin stimulates GnRH release; gonadorelin is GnRH. This is an upstream/downstream HPG-axis relationship.
rules.ai_action: Flag HPG-axis redundancy/load. Do not infer improved sex hormones without context and pulsatility logic.
hard_limits: Mark all non-direct combinations as inference

rules.name: Oxytocin + Vasopressin receptor-family cross-talk
rules.class_scope: Related posterior-pituitary nonapeptides
rules.evidence_basis: Oxytocin and vasopressin are related neuropeptides with overlapping social/stress contexts but vasopressin has strong water-balance/vascular actions.
rules.ai_action: Mark behavioral effects as context-dependent and add water-balance/BP flags when vasopressin is present.
hard_limits: Mark all non-direct combinations as inference

rules.name: Melanocortin + oxytocin/vasopressin CNS-social-sexual overlap
rules.class_scope: Melanocortin sexual behavior axis plus social neuropeptides
rules.evidence_basis: Both melanocortin and oxytocin/vasopressin systems participate in sexual/social/autonomic behavior networks, but direct clinical combination evidence is not established.
rules.ai_action: Add CNS/autonomic/behavioral overlap; avoid deterministic claims about libido or bonding.
hard_limits: Mark all non-direct combinations as inference

rules.name: Semax + Selank CNS peptide overlap
rules.class_scope: Experimental neuroactive peptides
rules.evidence_basis: Semax has ACTH-fragment/neurotrophic literature; Selank has anxiolytic/nootropic literature. Direct combination evidence is sparse.
rules.ai_action: Mark CNS effects as overlapping and evidence-limited; avoid claims of additive nootropic benefit.
hard_limits: Mark all non-direct combinations as inference

rules.name: DSIP with Semax/Selank or sedating agents
rules.class_scope: DSIP plus neuroactive peptides
rules.evidence_basis: DSIP evidence remains unresolved; combining with CNS-active peptides should be treated as unknown, not as a known sleep/anxiety stack.
rules.ai_action: Force 'unknown/insufficient evidence' unless direct study found.
hard_limits: Mark all non-direct combinations as inference

rules.name: Cerebrolysin with Semax/Selank neurotrophic overlap
rules.class_scope: Neurotrophic peptide mixture plus neuroactive peptides
rules.evidence_basis: Cerebrolysin is a peptide mixture with neurotrophic-like activity; Semax/Selank are defined peptides with heterogeneous neuroactive evidence.
rules.ai_action: Do not equate mechanisms. Mark as neurotrophic/CNS overlap with poor direct combination evidence.
hard_limits: Mark all non-direct combinations as inference

rules.name: GH/IGF axis + tissue-repair angiogenesis peptides
rules.class_scope: GH/IGF/proliferative axis plus repair/remodeling peptides
rules.evidence_basis: GH/IGF signaling is anabolic/growth-factor biology; BPC/TB4/GHK/MGF converge on repair/remodeling/angiogenesis/progenitor-cell signals. Direct stack evidence is lacking.
rules.ai_action: Flag growth/remodeling pathway load and absence of direct combination data.
hard_limits: Mark all non-direct combinations as inference

rules.name: GLP/amylin GI slowing with oral/absorption-sensitive agents
rules.class_scope: Agents that slow gastric emptying
rules.confidence: high mechanism; agent-specific clinical relevance varies
rules.evidence_basis: GLP-1 and amylin physiology can slow gastric emptying; this may matter for absorption-sensitive agents even if not peptide-peptide interaction.
rules.ai_action: When oral co-agents are present in a broader medication graph, flag possible absorption timing uncertainty without giving dosing instructions.
hard_limits: Mark all non-direct combinations as inference

rules.name: Insulin/pramlintide/GLP glycemic load rule
rules.class_scope: Amylin/GLP agents plus insulin or insulin secretagogue context
rules.evidence_basis: Pramlintide/amylin and GLP-1 agents lower postprandial glycemic excursions through glucagon, gastric emptying, satiety and insulin-related mechanisms; hypoglycemia risk is context-dependent especially with insulin/secretagogues.
rules.ai_action: If insulin or secretagogue appears in patient medication graph, raise glycemic-risk flag. No dosing instructions.
hard_limits: Mark all non-direct combinations as inference

rules.name: LL-37 + tissue-repair peptides in infected wound research context
rules.class_scope: Antimicrobial/wound peptide plus repair or anti-inflammatory peptides
rules.evidence_basis: LL-37 has antimicrobial/anti-biofilm/wound-healing literature; BPC/TB4/GHK/KPV have repair or anti-inflammatory signals. Direct combinations are not established.
rules.ai_action: Mark possible complementary wound biology but require direct-evidence=false and infection/inflammation context notes.
hard_limits: Mark all non-direct combinations as inference

rules.name: Epitalon/DSIP legacy peptide evidence caution
rules.class_scope: Legacy/heterogeneous peptide literature
rules.evidence_basis: Both have older or heterogeneous literatures with unresolved clinical mechanisms compared with stronger metabolic peptide evidence.
rules.ai_action: When either appears, downgrade certainty and prevent strong clinical claims.
hard_limits: Mark all non-direct combinations as inference

rules.name: Route/formulation identity warning for fragment/proxy peptides
rules.class_scope: Commercial/research fragments or formulations with evidence transferred from related molecules
rules.evidence_basis: TB-500 vs TB4, PEG-MGF vs MGF-E, topical vs injectable GHK-Cu, hGH fragments vs GH, and varied BPC-157 models are not always interchangeable.
rules.ai_action: Require exact molecule/formulation matching before applying evidence to an interaction.
hard_limits: Mark all non-direct combinations as inference

S01.title: GLP-1 mechanisms: insulin secretion, glucagon suppression, gastric emptying, food intake
supports: GLP-1 receptor agonism increases glucose-dependent insulin secretion, suppresses glucagon, slows gastric emptying, and reduces food intake
limits: Does not validate arbitrary stacking of GLP-1 agents

S02.title: GLP-1 biology review
supports: GLP-1 has metabolic effects including glucose-dependent insulin secretion, slower gastric emptying, and reduced food intake
limits: General biology; not a specific combination study

S03.title: Tirzepatide dual GIP/GLP-1 receptor co-agonist
supports: Tirzepatide is engineered to activate both GIP and GLP-1 receptors
limits: Does not establish safety or efficacy of tirzepatide with other incretin/amylin agents

S04.title: Retatrutide triple GIP/GLP-1/glucagon receptor agonist phase 2 trial
supports: Retatrutide is a GIP, GLP-1, and glucagon receptor agonist; GI adverse events and heart-rate signal were reported in the phase 2 obesity trial
limits: Does not test retatrutide stacked with semaglutide, tirzepatide, or cagrilintide

S05.title: Cagrilintide-semaglutide coadministration in obesity/type 2 diabetes
supports: Cagrilintide plus semaglutide has direct human trial evidence as a fixed combination; rationale is complementary amylin and GLP-1 mechanisms; GI adverse events were common
limits: Specific to studied combination and population; does not generalize to every amylin/GLP-1 stack

S06.title: Pramlintide/amylin mechanism review
supports: Amylin/pramlintide lowers postprandial glucagon, delays gastric emptying, and increases satiety
limits: Pramlintide evidence should not be automatically transferred to cagrilintide without agent-specific data

S07.title: AOD9604/hGH fragment metabolic effects in obese mice
supports: AOD9604/hGH C-terminal fragment increased fat oxidation/lipolysis in obese mice and did not interact with the hGH receptor in that study
limits: Preclinical animal evidence; not proof of human clinical efficacy

S08.title: CJC-1295 long-acting GHRH analog increases GH and IGF-1
supports: CJC-1295 produced sustained dose-dependent GH and IGF-1 increases in healthy adults
limits: Small pharmacology study; not long-term clinical outcomes

S09.title: Tesamorelin GHRH analogue effects on VAT and IGF-1 in HIV-associated abdominal fat
supports: Tesamorelin, a GHRH analogue, decreased visceral adipose tissue and increased IGF-1 in a randomized trial
limits: Population-specific; not evidence for general anti-aging or stacking with other GH secretagogues

S10.title: Ipamorelin selective GH secretagogue
supports: Ipamorelin released GH with little ACTH/cortisol effect compared with GHRP-2/GHRP-6 in the reported model
limits: Much of the selectivity evidence is preclinical; direct human clinical outcome evidence is limited

S11.title: GHRP-2 and hexarelin effects on GH, prolactin, ACTH and cortisol in humans
supports: GHRP-2 and hexarelin stimulate GH and can also increase prolactin, ACTH, and cortisol in humans
limits: Does not establish outcomes of multi-agent GH-secretagogue stacks

S12.title: Combined ghrelin and GHRH stimulate GH synergistically in humans
supports: Ghrelin plus GHRH produced synergistic GH release in humans
limits: Class inference is required when applying to synthetic GHSR agonists plus synthetic GHRH analogues

S13.title: Growth hormone effects on insulin signaling and glucose metabolism
supports: Chronic GH signaling can oppose insulin action through lipolysis/FFA flux and insulin signaling effects
limits: Does not quantify risk for any specific peptide combination

S14.title: GH/IGF/insulin axis and cancer biology
supports: GH, IGF-I and insulin have growth-promoting/anabolic actions and have been studied in relation to tumor promotion/progression
limits: Does not prove a direct clinical cancer risk for a given research-peptide stack

S15.title: MOTS-c promotes metabolic homeostasis and reduces obesity/insulin resistance in mice
supports: MOTS-c is a mitochondrial-derived peptide with preclinical metabolic effects involving obesity and insulin resistance models
limits: Predominantly preclinical; not proof of human clinical benefit or stacking safety

S16.title: SS-31/elamipretide mitochondrial membrane/cardiolipin interaction
supports: SS-31/elamipretide partitions into mitochondrial membrane interfaces and is linked to mitoprotective effects
limits: Mechanistic; not evidence for all longevity claims or combinations

S17.title: Humanin/mitochondrial-derived peptides regulate apoptosis, insulin sensitivity, inflammatory markers
supports: Humanin and related mitochondrial-derived peptides are linked to apoptosis, insulin sensitivity, and inflammatory-marker regulation
limits: Mostly mechanistic/preclinical; not direct combination evidence

S18.title: Epitalon overview and telomerase/telomere cell-line evidence
supports: Epitalon is a tetrapeptide studied for telomerase/telomere and aging-related biology, largely in experimental models
limits: Evidence is limited/heterogeneous; not a validated human clinical interaction base

S19.title: BPC-157 narrative/scoping review: investigational, clinical evidence limited
supports: BPC-157 evidence is primarily preclinical; review recommends caution until well-designed clinical trials exist
limits: Narrative/scoping review; some literature from same research groups

S20.title: BPC-157 wound/NO/angiogenesis literature
supports: BPC-157 has been linked in the literature to wound healing, NO-system effects, and angiogenesis-related mechanisms
limits: Mostly preclinical; does not establish human musculoskeletal efficacy

S21.title: Thymosin beta-4 accelerates wound healing in rat model
supports: Thymosin beta-4 enhanced wound healing in a rat full-thickness wound model
limits: Animal evidence; TB-500 commercial derivatives may not be identical to native TB4 evidence

S22.title: GHK/GHK-Cu tissue remodeling and wound healing
supports: GHK-Cu is associated with wound healing, skin repair, collagen turnover, and tissue remodeling
limits: Evidence varies by route/formulation/model; not general proof for injectable systemic use

S23.title: KPV anti-inflammatory effects in murine colitis models
supports: KPV showed anti-inflammatory effects in murine colitis models
limits: Preclinical disease models; direct human systemic interaction data are sparse

S24.title: Thymosin alpha-1 comprehensive immune-modulation review
supports: Thymosin alpha-1 is recognized as an immunomodulatory thymic peptide
limits: Clinical effects vary by disease context; not direct evidence for arbitrary immune stacks

S25.title: LL-37 antimicrobial and immunomodulatory peptide review
supports: LL-37 has broad antimicrobial, anti-biofilm, wound-healing and immunomodulatory roles
limits: Context-dependent biology; antimicrobial peptide activity does not equal safe systemic use

S26.title: LL-37 as a T-cell autoantigen in psoriasis
supports: LL-37 can be involved in autoimmune/inflammatory pathways such as psoriasis
limits: Disease-specific immunology; not a direct adverse-event prediction for every subject

S27.title: Bremelanotide/PT-141 melanocortin pathway and sexual desire
supports: Bremelanotide acts through melanocortin receptor pathways; proposed CNS mechanism includes MC4R-related dopaminergic sexual desire signaling
limits: Does not establish direct interaction with kisspeptin, oxytocin, or other agents

S28.title: Kisspeptin directly stimulates GnRH/LH pathway
supports: Kisspeptin directly stimulates GnRH release, driving gonadotropin signaling
limits: Physiology evidence does not equal clinical outcome from stacking with GnRH analogues

S29.title: GnRH physiology and pulsatile LH/FSH regulation
supports: GnRH pulsatility regulates LH and FSH synthesis/secretion patterns
limits: Reference physiology; not a peptide-stack trial

S30.title: Oxytocin and vasopressin in social behavior and physiology
supports: Oxytocin and vasopressin are related neuropeptides involved in social behavior, stress, reproductive and water-balance physiology
limits: Effects are context-dependent; not direct evidence for combination protocols

S31.title: Semax ACTH fragment analog and BDNF/neuroprotection literature
supports: Semax is an ACTH(4-10)/ACTH(4-7)-related synthetic peptide studied for neurotrophic/neuroprotective signals such as BDNF in animal models
limits: Evidence base is regionally concentrated and largely preclinical/heterogeneous

S32.title: Selank molecular aspects and anxiolytic/nootropic effects
supports: Selank is a heptapeptide studied for prolonged anti-anxiety and nootropic effects
limits: Direct high-quality human interaction evidence is sparse

S33.title: DSIP unresolved evidence base
supports: Delta sleep-inducing peptide remains biologically and clinically unresolved
limits: Older heterogeneous evidence; no reliable basis for strong AI recommendations

S34.title: Cerebrolysin peptide mixture mimics neurotrophic factor actions in models
supports: Cerebrolysin is a peptide preparation with neurotrophic-like effects studied in neurodegeneration/stroke models
limits: Mixture product; mechanism and evidence are not interchangeable with single defined peptides

S35.title: Mechano growth factor/MGF-E evidence and contradictory findings
supports: MGF-E peptide has been studied in human muscle progenitor cells; reports exist for activation/fusion potential
limits: PEG-MGF specifically has very limited direct peer-reviewed evidence; MGF findings are not necessarily transferable
